name: cc-harness
description: cc-harness — a five-layer harness for Claude Code, each layer built on the one below. 1) cc-config sets it up. 2) cc-tools gives you the primitives. 3) cc-pipeline is the build pipeline that carries a task from idea to hardened code. 4) cc-worker is the second pilot that drives the pipeline on one project. 5) cc-chief is the top brain that orchestrates workers across a product's sub-projects. Install cc-config first.

plugins.name: cc-config
plugins.description: Layer 1/5 — setup. The front desk that installs and maintains the harness itself. /cc-init walks you through onboarding: recommended rules, the cheat-sheet reminder hook, and a stale-docs check. Commands only — you run them, the model can't. Install this first; it bootstraps the other four layers.

plugins.name: cc-tools
plugins.description: Layer 2/5 — primitives. Small, project-agnostic skills you or the model reach for while working: /slap resets a stuck fix, /likec4 draws C4 architecture-as-code, /reminder pins one rule file as a hard constraint, plus cctreectl to map the project tree. Skills only, zero config. The primitives every layer above builds on.

plugins.name: cc-pipeline
plugins.description: Layer 3/5 — the build pipeline. Six stage skills that carry one task from a direction to hardened code: /what-to-do (where to go next), /how-to-do (decide a fork), /do (build it by a tight set of writing rules), then /refactor, /review, /test to harden and ship it. Skills only. Built on cc-tools; driven by cc-worker.

plugins.name: cc-worker
plugins.description: Layer 4/5 — the second pilot. Hand /worker any piece of work (a fix, a change, a feature, a question) and it adapts: reads the goal (roadmap) and design (architecture), then drives the right cc-pipeline and cc-tools skills to finish it. Runs inline as /worker or as a native subagent you delegate a whole task to. Also owns the roadmap (/planner-brainstorm) and architecture (/sysdesign-brainstorm). Built on cc-pipeline + cc-tools.

plugins.name: cc-chief
plugins.description: Layer 5/5 — the top brain. /chief (backed by the chief-rules skill) orchestrates the workers across a product's sub-projects, each its own git repo under the current folder. It owns the product plane — the product-wide roadmap of cross-cutting epics (roadmap-management) and the product CLAUDE.md (via /chief-init) — keeps each repo's roadmap true, and sequences and dispatches workers (as native subagents) where cross-repo work needs them. It conducts; the workers build. Built on cc-worker.
